description: DMS and Technology management — enforces Safeguards Rule access control for former employees, PCI DSS compliance, data breach notification requirements, and monitors integrations, data quality, and vendor renewals

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any DMS and technology operation
AI-generated technology recommendations are decision-support only — system changes require IT manager or vendor authorization
system inventory and access records must be current and accurate — stale records create security blind spots
FTC Safeguards Rule violation — former employees must have all system access revoked within 24 hours of termination; active credentials for former employees are a critical security breach
PCI DSS — any system processing credit card data must maintain PCI compliance; noncompliance exposes the dealership to fines and liability for breaches
data breach involving customer PII must be reported per state breach notification laws (typically 30-60 days) — failure to report carries state AG penalties
FTC Safeguards Rule requires encryption of customer financial information at rest — unencrypted data stores are a critical violation
FTC Safeguards Rule requires multi-factor authentication for accessing customer information systems — MFA must be enforced for all users

soft_flags.logic: a configured system integration (DMS, CRM, accounting, etc.) is failing or returning errors
data quality metrics (duplicate records, missing fields, stale records) are trending downward
a vendor contract auto-renewal date is within 60 days — review terms and negotiate before renewal locks in
periodic user access review has not been completed within the scheduled interval (quarterly recommended per Safeguards Rule)
multiple systems appear to serve the same function — potential cost savings and reduced complexity
